subject: Project Management
title: Scrum at Amazon
description: A short lesson to help you apply Scrum at Amazon.

hook.text: Jeff Bezos had a rule: every team should be small enough to feed with two pizzas.

buildup.text: Amazon's 'two-pizza teams' are 6-10 people, each owning a service end-to-end.

discovery.text: These autonomous teams operate like internal startups — they build, deploy, and support their own code.

twist.text: Amazon never formally adopted Scrum, but its structure mirrors Scrum's ideals at massive scale.

climax.text: Small, autonomous teams with clear ownership — Scrum's dream, Amazon's reality.

punchline.text: Scrum's best idea needed no Scrum certification.

quiz.question: What is Amazon's 'two-pizza team' rule?
options: Teams should be small enough to feed with two pizzas
Every team gets two pizza lunches per week
